title: The Capital Falls Into Chaos
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on the capital falls into chaos, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Capital Falls Into Chaos. Alubarna becomes the battlefield where Koza, Cobra’s army, and Crocodile’s manipulation finally meet. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: And because she can't turn a blind eye to anybody,

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Even for this rebellion, she's wishing for nobody to die.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Native pacifists like her always think such deluded things... They don't understand the true nature of war.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You agree with me, don't you?

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: ...But she'll continue to risk her life and fight as long as you're around,

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: So I'm gonna stop you here and now.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: I've tossed aside count- less of fools like you,

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: Who'd die for the sake of friendship...!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I guess that'd make you the stupid one!

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: Is there something you find humorous!?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Would you like to die as well, nice Robin?

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: In any case, didn't you promise me not to call me by that name?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: I'm going on ahead to Alubarma.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: I can´t play around with you any longer than that.

summary: This part develops the capital falls into chaos and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
